title: Exclude the Third
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro · native, interactive · a real seeded stochastic-resonance run (resonance.py → data.json, stdlib only, reproducible) displayed by a CSP-clean viewer; the reader turns a noise knob and watches a subthreshold message appear and die
embodies: The swerve is Michel Serres, Le Parasite (1980): the parasite is also the static on the line — the excluded third that 'a successful communication' expels. A DETECTOR is exactly this apparatus of exclusion; S34's mirror-machine 'optimising away inaccurate citations' is Serres' war on the third. The deflection (n-1, subtract): there are channels where excluding the third destroys the message. The physical case is stochastic resonance (Gammaitoni et al., Rev. Mod. Phys. 70, 223, 1998) — a signal too weak to cross a threshold (A=0.30 < Θ=0.50) is carried over ONLY by added noise, and only at an intermediate amount: at D=0 the clean channel is silent (0 spikes, ran for real), transmission peaks near D=0.22, then the parasite eats the host. The carrier and the killer are the SAME third in different amounts. This gives S34's finding a body: a level-counting detector cannot tell the degradation regime (clean is better — the collapse works 03/11/12/14) from the constitutive regime (some third is necessary) from the output alone, exactly as the citation checker cannot tell the hidden lie from the disclosed gap — to a detector both are the third man. Reliability (a quiet channel) and honesty (which third you kept) are different quantities.
